cards.label: 01 · pre-book
cards.title: 25 June 2026 — set an alarm.
cards.body: Pre-orders open globally on Wednesday 25 June at rockstargames.com/VI plus PlayStation Store and Xbox Store. India retailers (Amazon India, Flipkart, Games The Shop) typically follow within 24-48 hours. Collector's editions sold out in under 6 hours at Rockstar's last pre-order — set an alarm.

cards.label: 02 · launch
cards.title: November 19, 2026 — PS5 + Xbox Series.
cards.body: Rockstar Games confirmed the launch date. PC release expected 2027 (Rockstar's usual ~12-month delay pattern). No paid early access — the game ships on Day 1 for everyone, everywhere.

cards.label: 02 · world
cards.title: Vice City returns. Two protagonists.
cards.body: Set in Leonida, a fictional Florida state. Two playable leads — Lucia (first female lead in the series) and Jason. Largest GTA map ever. Bonnie-and-Clyde-style narrative confirmed in the December 2023 trailer.

cards.label: 03 · budget
cards.title: Rumored $1B-$2B development cost.
cards.body: Most expensive game ever made. Spread across 7+ years, 2,000+ Rockstar employees across 6 studios, motion capture, voice acting, music licensing, pre-launch marketing.

cards.label: 04 · tech
cards.title: Built on RAGE + Euphoria.
cards.body: Rockstar Advanced Game Engine — proprietary, rebuilt for GTA VI with new lighting, weather, water physics, NPC behavior. Euphoria layer handles procedural character animation. None of it is open-source.

cards.label: 05 · diy
cards.title: Want to build a game without $2B?
cards.body: The 2026 open-source stack gets a solo dev to a playable prototype in weeks: Godot 4.5 (free, lightweight), Unreal Engine 5 (free until $1M revenue), Blender for 3D assets, Three.js for browser games, ConVai or Inworld for AI NPCs.

take.use_if: You're excited for GTA VI AND curious how the tech behind games at this scale works — we cover both in the full blog.
take.skip_if: You're not into gaming. The technical breakdown won't be useful unless you're a builder.
